title: astragalus impensus
intro: Astragalus impensus, also known as the impensate milkvetch, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the pea family (Fabaceae). It is native to the mountainous regions of North America and is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and immunomodulatory effects. The plant's roots and leaves have been traditionally used to treat a range of health issues, including respiratory problems, fatigue, and stress, and are believed to promote overall well-being and longevity. Astragalus impensus is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or capsule, and is also used in combination with other herbs in traditional Chinese medicine to enhance its health benefits.
